var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

args.code:
import json
import os

# Hardcoded children's books list
childrens_books = [
    {"book_id": "bookid_4", "title": "Monstrous Stories #4: The Day the Mice Stood Still"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_14", "title": "The Old Man and the Pirate Princess"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_32", "title": "The Very Hungry Caterpillar (English and Arabic Edition)"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_40", "title": "Egypt (Enchantment of the World)"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_48", "title": "Clark the Shark: Tooth Trouble, No. 1"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_52", "title": "I Hadn't Meant to Tell You This"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_54", "title": "Favorite Thorton W. Burgess Stories: 6 Books"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_55", "title": "Behind the Wheel (Choose Your Own Adventure #35)(Paperback/Revised)"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_57", "title": "Goodnight Riley and the Moon, It's Almost Bedtime"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_96", "title": "Cheer Up, Ben Franklin! (Young Historians)"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_99", "title": "Buddy the Soldier Bear"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_108", "title": "The Library Book"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_111", "title": "Can You Buy Me The Wind"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_116", "title": "From the Earth to the Moon by Jules Verne"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_120", "title": "Caroline's Color Dreams"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_124", "title": "Skeleton Play: A Fun, Rhyming Halloween Book"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_130", "title": "Benny Goes To The Moon"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_132", "title": "Mae and Jane in the Rain"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_145", "title": "Kirsten: An American Girl"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_146", "title": "LunaLu the Llamacorn"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_149", "title": "Trouble in the CTC"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_152", "title": "Around the World Mazes"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_158", "title": "Cleo Porter and the Body Electric"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_163", "title": "Monster Kisses"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_170", "title": "Pokemon: Sun and Moon, Vol. 8"}
]

childrens_book_ids = {book['book_id'] for book in childrens_books}
book_titles = {book['book_id']: book['title'] for book in childrens_books}

# Find the reviews file by checking the variable name pattern
for key, value in globals().items():
    if 'query_db' in key and 'review' in str(value):
        if isinstance(value, str) and os.path.exists(value):
            reviews_file = value
            break
else:
    # Check common locations
    potential_files = [f for f in os.listdir('/tmp') if f.startswith('tmp') and f.endswith('.json')]
    if potential_files:
        reviews_file = os.path.join('/tmp', potential_files[0])
    else:
        print('Could not find reviews file')
        qualified_books = []
        print('__RESULT__:')
        print(json.dumps(qualified_books))
        exit()

print('Using reviews file:', reviews_file)

with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

print('Total reviews loaded:', len(reviews))

# Filter reviews
reviews_2020_childrens = []
for review in reviews:
    if review.get('review_time', '') >= '2020-01-01':
        try:
            purchase_num = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
            book_id = 'bookid_' + purchase_num
            if book_id in childrens_book_ids:
                reviews_2020_childrens.append({
                    'book_id': book_id,
                    'rating': float(review['rating'])
                })
        except (IndexError, ValueError, KeyError):
            continue

print('Children\'s book reviews from 2020 onwards:', len(reviews_2020_childrens))

# Calculate average ratings
from collections import defaultdict
book_ratings = defaultdict(list)
for review in reviews_2020_childrens:
    book_ratings[review['book_id']].append(review['rating'])

qualified_books = []
for book_id, ratings in book_ratings.items():
    avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    if avg_rating >= 4.5:
        qualified_books.append({
            'book_id': book_id,
            'title': book_titles.get(book_id, 'Unknown'),
            'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 2),
            'review_count': len(ratings)
        })

qualified_books.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('Qualified books:', qualified_books)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(qualified_books, ensure_ascii=False))

val_args.code:
import json
import os

# Hardcoded children's books list
childrens_books = [
    {"book_id": "bookid_4", "title": "Monstrous Stories #4: The Day the Mice Stood Still"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_14", "title": "The Old Man and the Pirate Princess"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_32", "title": "The Very Hungry Caterpillar (English and Arabic Edition)"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_40", "title": "Egypt (Enchantment of the World)"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_48", "title": "Clark the Shark: Tooth Trouble, No. 1"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_52", "title": "I Hadn't Meant to Tell You This"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_54", "title": "Favorite Thorton W. Burgess Stories: 6 Books"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_55", "title": "Behind the Wheel (Choose Your Own Adventure #35)(Paperback/Revised)"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_57", "title": "Goodnight Riley and the Moon, It's Almost Bedtime"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_96", "title": "Cheer Up, Ben Franklin! (Young Historians)"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_99", "title": "Buddy the Soldier Bear"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_108", "title": "The Library Book"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_111", "title": "Can You Buy Me The Wind"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_116", "title": "From the Earth to the Moon by Jules Verne"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_120", "title": "Caroline's Color Dreams"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_124", "title": "Skeleton Play: A Fun, Rhyming Halloween Book"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_130", "title": "Benny Goes To The Moon"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_132", "title": "Mae and Jane in the Rain"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_145", "title": "Kirsten: An American Girl"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_146", "title": "LunaLu the Llamacorn"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_149", "title": "Trouble in the CTC"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_152", "title": "Around the World Mazes"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_158", "title": "Cleo Porter and the Body Electric"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_163", "title": "Monster Kisses"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_170", "title": "Pokemon: Sun and Moon, Vol. 8"}
]

childrens_book_ids = {book['book_id'] for book in childrens_books}
book_titles = {book['book_id']: book['title'] for book in childrens_books}

# Find the reviews file by checking the variable name pattern
for key, value in globals().items():
    if 'query_db' in key and 'review' in str(value):
        if isinstance(value, str) and os.path.exists(value):
            reviews_file = value
            break
else:
    # Check common locations
    potential_files = [f for f in os.listdir('/tmp') if f.startswith('tmp') and f.endswith('.json')]
    if potential_files:
        reviews_file = os.path.join('/tmp', potential_files[0])
    else:
        print('Could not find reviews file')
        qualified_books = []
        print('__RESULT__:')
        print(json.dumps(qualified_books))
        exit()

print('Using reviews file:', reviews_file)

with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

print('Total reviews loaded:', len(reviews))

# Filter reviews
reviews_2020_childrens = []
for review in reviews:
    if review.get('review_time', '') >= '2020-01-01':
        try:
            purchase_num = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
            book_id = 'bookid_' + purchase_num
            if book_id in childrens_book_ids:
                reviews_2020_childrens.append({
                    'book_id': book_id,
                    'rating': float(review['rating'])
                })
        except (IndexError, ValueError, KeyError):
            continue

print('Children\'s book reviews from 2020 onwards:', len(reviews_2020_childrens))

# Calculate average ratings
from collections import defaultdict
book_ratings = defaultdict(list)
for review in reviews_2020_childrens:
    book_ratings[review['book_id']].append(review['rating'])

qualified_books = []
for book_id, ratings in book_ratings.items():
    avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    if avg_rating >= 4.5:
        qualified_books.append({
            'book_id': book_id,
            'title': book_titles.get(book_id, 'Unknown'),
            'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 2),
            'review_count': len(ratings)
        })

qualified_books.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('Qualified books:', qualified_books)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(qualified_books, ensure_ascii=False))

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict
import os

childrens_books = [
    {"book_id": "bookid_4", "title": "Monstrous Stories"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_14", "title": "The Old Man and the Pirate Princess"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_32", "title": "The Very Hungry Caterpillar"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_40", "title": "Egypt"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_48", "title": "Clark the Shark"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_52", "title": "I Hadn't Meant to Tell You This"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_54", "title": "Favorite Thorton W. Burgess Stories"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_55", "title": "Behind the Wheel"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_57", "title": "Goodnight Riley"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_96", "title": "Cheer Up, Ben Franklin"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_99", "title": "Buddy the Soldier Bear"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_108", "title": "The Library Book"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_111", "title": "Can You Buy Me The Wind"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_116", "title": "From the Earth to the Moon"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_120", "title": "Caroline's Color Dreams"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_124", "title": "Skeleton Play"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_130", "title": "Benny Goes To The Moon"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_132", "title": "Mae and Jane in the Rain"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_145", "title": "Kirsten: An American Girl"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_146", "title": "LunaLu the Llamacorn"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_149", "title": "Trouble in the CTC"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_152", "title": "Around the World Mazes"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_158", "title": "Cleo Porter"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_163", "title": "Monster Kisses"},
    {"book_id": "bookid_170", "title": "Pokemon Sun and Moon"}
]

childrens_book_ids = set(book["book_id"] for book in childrens_books)
book_titles = {book["book_id"]: book["title"] for book in childrens_books}

# Load reviews from file that should be in var_functions.query_db_25
review_key = "var_functions.query_db_25"
if review_key not in globals():
    print("Review file path not found")
    qualified_books = []
else:
    reviews_file = globals()[review_key]
    with open(reviews_file, "r") as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
    
    print("Total reviews:", len(reviews))
    
    # Filter reviews
    reviews_filtered = []
    for review in reviews:
        if review["review_time"] >= "2020-01-01":
            try:
                purchase_num = review["purchase_id"].split("_")[1]
                book_id = "bookid_" + purchase_num
                if book_id in childrens_book_ids:
                    reviews_filtered.append({
                        "book_id": book_id,
                        "rating": float(review["rating"])
                    })
            except:
                continue
    
    print("Children's reviews from 2020:", len(reviews_filtered))
    
    # Calculate average ratings
    book_ratings = defaultdict(list)
    for review in reviews_filtered:
        book_ratings[review["book_id"]].append(review["rating"])
    
    qualified_books = []
    for book_id, ratings in book_ratings.items():
        avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
        if avg_rating >= 4.5:
            qualified_books.append({
                "book_id": book_id,
                "title": book_titles.get(book_id, "Unknown"),
                "avg_rating": round(avg_rating, 2),
                "review_count": len(ratings)
            })
    
    qualified_books.sort(key=lambda x: x["avg_rating"], reverse=True)
    
    print("Qualified books found:", len(qualified_books))
    for book in qualified_books:
        print(f"  - {book['title']}: {book['avg_rating']} ({book['review_count']} reviews)")

# Print result in required format
result_string = json.dumps(qualified_books, ensure_ascii=False)
print("__RESULT__:")
print(result_string)

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Load children's books
childrens_books = [{"book_id": "bookid_4", "title": "Monstrous Stories #4: The Day the Mice Stood Still"}, {"book_id": "bookid_14", "title": "The Old Man and the Pirate Princess"}, {"book_id": "bookid_32", "title": "The Very Hungry Caterpillar (English and Arabic Edition)"}, {"book_id": "bookid_40", "title": "Egypt (Enchantment of the World)"}, {"book_id": "bookid_48", "title": "Clark the Shark: Tooth Trouble, No. 1"}, {"book_id": "bookid_52", "title": "I Hadn't Meant to Tell You This"}, {"book_id": "bookid_54", "title": "Favorite Thorton W. Burgess Stories: 6 Books"}, {"book_id": "bookid_55", "title": "Behind the Wheel (Choose Your Own Adventure #35)(Paperback/Revised)"}, {"book_id": "bookid_57", "title": "Goodnight Riley and the Moon, It's Almost Bedtime: Personalized Children Books"}, {"book_id": "bookid_96", "title": "Cheer Up, Ben Franklin! (Young Historians)"}, {"book_id": "bookid_99", "title": "Buddy the Soldier Bear"}, {"book_id": "bookid_108", "title": "The Library Book"}, {"book_id": "bookid_111", "title": "Can You Buy Me The Wind"}, {"book_id": "bookid_116", "title": "From the Earth to the Moon by Jules Verne"}, {"book_id": "bookid_120", "title": "Caroline's Color Dreams"}, {"book_id": "bookid_124", "title": "Skeleton Play: A Fun, Rhyming Halloween Book"}, {"book_id": "bookid_130", "title": "Benny Goes To The Moon"}, {"book_id": "bookid_132", "title": "Mae and Jane in the Rain"}, {"book_id": "bookid_145", "title": "Kirsten: An American Girl"}, {"book_id": "bookid_146", "title": "LunaLu the Llamacorn"}, {"book_id": "bookid_149", "title": "Trouble in the CTC"}, {"book_id": "bookid_152", "title": "Around the World Mazes"}, {"book_id": "bookid_158", "title": "Cleo Porter"}, {"book_id": "bookid_163", "title": "Monster Kisses"}, {"book_id": "bookid_170", "title": "Pokemon Sun and Moon"}]

childrens_book_ids = set(book["book_id"] for book in childrens_books)
book_titles = {book["book_id"]: book["title"] for book in childrens_books}

# Load reviews - variable name is var_functions.query_db_52 (from tool call id 52)
if "var_functions.query_db_52" not in globals():
    print("Reviews file not found")
    qualified_books = []
else:
    reviews_file = globals()["var_functions.query_db_52"]
    with open(reviews_file, "r") as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
    
    # Filter and process reviews
    reviews_2020_childrens = []
    for review in reviews:
        if review["review_time"] >= "2020-01-01":
            # Map purchase_id to book_id
            purchase_id = review["purchase_id"]
            # purchase_id format: purchaseid_X, book_id format: bookid_X
            purchase_num = purchase_id.split("_")[1]
            book_id = "bookid_" + purchase_num
            
            if book_id in childrens_book_ids:
                reviews_2020_childrens.append({
                    "book_id": book_id,
                    "rating": float(review["rating"])
                })
    
    # Calculate average ratings per book
    book_ratings = defaultdict(list)
    for review in reviews_2020_childrens:
        book_ratings[review["book_id"]].append(review["rating"])
    
    qualified_books = []
    for book_id, ratings in book_ratings.items():
        avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
        if avg_rating >= 4.5:
            qualified_books.append({
                "book_id": book_id,
                "title": book_titles.get(book_id, "Unknown"),
                "avg_rating": round(avg_rating, 2),
                "review_count": len(ratings)
            })
    
    qualified_books.sort(key=lambda x: x["avg_rating"], reverse=True)

# Format final answer
if qualified_books:
    answer_text = "The following children's books have an average rating of at least 4.5 based on reviews from 2020 onwards:\n\n"
    for book in qualified_books:
        answer_text += f"Title: {book['title']}\n"
        answer_text += f"Book ID: {book['book_id']}\n"
        answer_text += f"Average Rating: {book['avg_rating']}\n"
        answer_text += f"Number of Reviews: {book['review_count']}\n\n"
else:
    answer_text = "No children's books found with an average rating of at least 4.5 based on reviews from 2020 onwards."

print(answer_text)

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Load children's books
childrens_books = [{"book_id": "bookid_4", "title": "Monstrous Stories #4: The Day the Mice Stood Still"}, {"book_id": "bookid_14", "title": "The Old Man and the Pirate Princess"}, {"book_id": "bookid_32", "title": "The Very Hungry Caterpillar (English and Arabic Edition)"}, {"book_id": "bookid_40", "title": "Egypt (Enchantment of the World)"}, {"book_id": "bookid_48", "title": "Clark the Shark: Tooth Trouble, No. 1"}, {"book_id": "bookid_52", "title": "I Hadn't Meant to Tell You This"}, {"book_id": "bookid_54", "title": "Favorite Thorton W. Burgess Stories: 6 Books"}, {"book_id": "bookid_55", "title": "Behind the Wheel (Choose Your Own Adventure #35)(Paperback/Revised)"}, {"book_id": "bookid_57", "title": "Goodnight Riley and the Moon, It's Almost Bedtime: Personalized Children Books"}, {"book_id": "bookid_96", "title": "Cheer Up, Ben Franklin! (Young Historians)"}, {"book_id": "bookid_99", "title": "Buddy the Soldier Bear"}, {"book_id": "bookid_108", "title": "The Library Book"}, {"book_id": "bookid_111", "title": "Can You Buy Me The Wind"}, {"book_id": "bookid_116", "title": "From the Earth to the Moon by Jules Verne"}, {"book_id": "bookid_120", "title": "Caroline's Color Dreams"}, {"book_id": "bookid_124", "title": "Skeleton Play: A Fun, Rhyming Halloween Book"}, {"book_id": "bookid_130", "title": "Benny Goes To The Moon"}, {"book_id": "bookid_132", "title": "Mae and Jane in the Rain"}, {"book_id": "bookid_145", "title": "Kirsten: An American Girl"}, {"book_id": "bookid_146", "title": "LunaLu the Llamacorn"}, {"book_id": "bookid_149", "title": "Trouble in the CTC"}, {"book_id": "bookid_152", "title": "Around the World Mazes"}, {"book_id": "bookid_158", "title": "Cleo Porter"}, {"book_id": "bookid_163", "title": "Monster Kisses"}, {"book_id": "bookid_170", "title": "Pokemon Sun and Moon"}]

childrens_book_ids = set(book["book_id"] for book in childrens_books)
book_titles = {book["book_id"]: book["title"] for book in childrens_books}

# Load reviews - variable name is var_functions.query_db_52 (from tool call id 52)
if "var_functions.query_db_52" not in globals():
    print("Reviews file not found")
    qualified_books = []
else:
    reviews_file = globals()["var_functions.query_db_52"]
    with open(reviews_file, "r") as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
    
    # Filter and process reviews
    reviews_2020_childrens = []
    for review in reviews:
        if review["review_time"] >= "2020-01-01":
            # Map purchase_id to book_id
            purchase_id = review["purchase_id"]
            # purchase_id format: purchaseid_X, book_id format: bookid_X
            purchase_num = purchase_id.split("_")[1]
            book_id = "bookid_" + purchase_num
            
            if book_id in childrens_book_ids:
                reviews_2020_childrens.append({
                    "book_id": book_id,
                    "rating": float(review["rating"])
                })
    
    # Calculate average ratings per book
    book_ratings = defaultdict(list)
    for review in reviews_2020_childrens:
        book_ratings[review["book_id"]].append(review["rating"])
    
    qualified_books = []
    for book_id, ratings in book_ratings.items():
        avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
        if avg_rating >= 4.5:
            qualified_books.append({
                "book_id": book_id,
                "title": book_titles.get(book_id, "Unknown"),
                "avg_rating": round(avg_rating, 2),
                "review_count": len(ratings)
            })
    
    qualified_books.sort(key=lambda x: x["avg_rating"], reverse=True)

# Format final answer
if qualified_books:
    answer_text = "The following children's books have an average rating of at least 4.5 based on reviews from 2020 onwards:\n\n"
    for book in qualified_books:
        answer_text += f"Title: {book['title']}\n"
        answer_text += f"Book ID: {book['book_id']}\n"
        answer_text += f"Average Rating: {book['avg_rating']}\n"
        answer_text += f"Number of Reviews: {book['review_count']}\n\n"
else:
    answer_text = "No children's books found with an average rating of at least 4.5 based on reviews from 2020 onwards."

print(answer_text)

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Get children's books data from the query result
childrens_books_data = [
    {"book_id":"bookid_4","title":"Monstrous Stories"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_14","title":"The Old Man and the Pirate Princess"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_32","title":"The Very Hungry Caterpillar"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_40","title":"Egypt"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_48","title":"Clark the Shark"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_52","title":"I Hadn't Meant to Tell You This"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_54","title":"Favorite Thorton W. Burgess Stories"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_55","title":"Behind the Wheel"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_57","title":"Goodnight Riley"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_96","title":"Cheer Up, Ben Franklin"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_99","title":"Buddy the Soldier Bear"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_108","title":"The Library Book"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_111","title":"Can You Buy Me The Wind"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_116","title":"From the Earth to the Moon"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_120","title":"Caroline's Color Dreams"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_124","title":"Skeleton Play"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_130","title":"Benny Goes To The Moon"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_132","title":"Mae and Jane in the Rain"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_145","title":"Kirsten: An American Girl"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_146","title":"LunaLu the Llamacorn"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_149","title":"Trouble in the CTC"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_152","title":"Around the World Mazes"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_158","title":"Cleo Porter"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_163","title":"Monster Kisses"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_170","title":"Pokemon Sun and Moon"}
]

childrens_book_ids = set(book["book_id"] for book in childrens_books_data)
book_titles = {book["book_id"]: book["title"] for book in childrens_books_data}

# Load reviews from the query result stored in var_functions.query_db_25
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db_25
with open(reviews_file, "r") as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Filter reviews and calculate averages
book_ratings = defaultdict(list)
for review in reviews:
    # Check if review is from 2020 onwards
    if review["review_time"] >= "2020-01-01":
        purchase_id = review["purchase_id"]
        purchase_num = purchase_id.split("_")[1]
        book_id = "bookid_" + purchase_num
        
        if book_id in childrens_book_ids:
            book_ratings[book_id].append(float(review["rating"]))

qualified_books = []
for book_id, ratings in book_ratings.items():
    avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    if avg_rating >= 4.5:
        qualified_books.append({
            "book_id": book_id,
            "title": book_titles.get(book_id, "Unknown"),
            "avg_rating": round(avg_rating, 2),
            "review_count": len(ratings)
        })

qualified_books.sort(key=lambda x: x["avg_rating"], reverse=True)

# Generate answer based on results
if qualified_books:
    answer_text = "The following children's books have an average rating of at least 4.5 based on reviews from 2020 onwards:\n\n"
    for book in qualified_books:
        answer_text += "Title: " + book["title"] + "\n"
        answer_text += "Book ID: " + book["book_id"] + "\n"
        answer_text += "Average Rating: " + str(book["avg_rating"]) + "\n"
        answer_text += "Number of Reviews: " + str(book["review_count"]) + "\n\n"
else:
    answer_text = "No children's books found with an average rating of at least 4.5 based on reviews from 2020 onwards."

print(answer_text)

Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Get children's books data from the query result
childrens_books_data = [
    {"book_id":"bookid_4","title":"Monstrous Stories"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_14","title":"The Old Man and the Pirate Princess"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_32","title":"The Very Hungry Caterpillar"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_40","title":"Egypt"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_48","title":"Clark the Shark"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_52","title":"I Hadn't Meant to Tell You This"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_54","title":"Favorite Thorton W. Burgess Stories"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_55","title":"Behind the Wheel"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_57","title":"Goodnight Riley"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_96","title":"Cheer Up, Ben Franklin"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_99","title":"Buddy the Soldier Bear"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_108","title":"The Library Book"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_111","title":"Can You Buy Me The Wind"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_116","title":"From the Earth to the Moon"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_120","title":"Caroline's Color Dreams"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_124","title":"Skeleton Play"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_130","title":"Benny Goes To The Moon"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_132","title":"Mae and Jane in the Rain"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_145","title":"Kirsten: An American Girl"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_146","title":"LunaLu the Llamacorn"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_149","title":"Trouble in the CTC"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_152","title":"Around the World Mazes"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_158","title":"Cleo Porter"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_163","title":"Monster Kisses"},
    {"book_id":"bookid_170","title":"Pokemon Sun and Moon"}
]

childrens_book_ids = set(book["book_id"] for book in childrens_books_data)
book_titles = {book["book_id"]: book["title"] for book in childrens_books_data}

# Load reviews from the query result stored in var_functions.query_db_25
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db_25
with open(reviews_file, "r") as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Filter reviews and calculate averages
book_ratings = defaultdict(list)
for review in reviews:
    # Check if review is from 2020 onwards
    if review["review_time"] >= "2020-01-01":
        purchase_id = review["purchase_id"]
        purchase_num = purchase_id.split("_")[1]
        book_id = "bookid_" + purchase_num
        
        if book_id in childrens_book_ids:
            book_ratings[book_id].append(float(review["rating"]))

qualified_books = []
for book_id, ratings in book_ratings.items():
    avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    if avg_rating >= 4.5:
        qualified_books.append({
            "book_id": book_id,
            "title": book_titles.get(book_id, "Unknown"),
            "avg_rating": round(avg_rating, 2),
            "review_count": len(ratings)
        })

qualified_books.sort(key=lambda x: x["avg_rating"], reverse=True)

# Generate answer based on results
if qualified_books:
    answer_text = "The following children's books have an average rating of at least 4.5 based on reviews from 2020 onwards:\n\n"
    for book in qualified_books:
        answer_text += "Title: " + book["title"] + "\n"
        answer_text += "Book ID: " + book["book_id"] + "\n"
        answer_text += "Average Rating: " + str(book["avg_rating"]) + "\n"
        answer_text += "Number of Reviews: " + str(book["review_count"]) + "\n\n"
else:
    answer_text = "No children's books found with an average rating of at least 4.5 based on reviews from 2020 onwards."

print(answer_text)

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

args.code:
import json
from collections import defaultdict

# Load data from storage variables
books = globals()['var_functions.query_db_50']
reviews_file = globals()['var_functions.query_db_52']

# Load reviews
import json as js
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = js.load(f)

# Process data
childrens_ids = {b['book_id'] for b in books}
book_titles = {b['book_id']: b['title'] for b in books}

ratings = defaultdict(list)
for r in reviews:
    if r['review_time'] >= '2020-01-01':
        book_id = 'bookid_' + r['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
        if book_id in childrens_ids:
            ratings[book_id].append(float(r['rating']))

qualified = []
for book_id, rating_list in ratings.items():
    avg = sum(rating_list) / len(rating_list)
    if avg >= 4.5:
        qualified.append({
            'book_id': book_id,
            'title': book_titles.get(book_id, 'Unknown'),
            'avg_rating': round(avg, 2),
            'review_count': len(rating_list)
        })

qualified.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

# Format output
output = []
for book in qualified:
    output.append(book)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(js.dumps(output))

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:10.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:10.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:10.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!
